title: Auth and Tenant CLI Commands

story.i_want_to: log in to a loopctl server, view my identity, and manage my tenant registration and settings via the CLI
story.so_that: I can set up and configure my loopctl tenant without needing to make raw API calls

rationale: Authentication and tenant management are the first actions any user performs after installing the CLI. The auth login command stores credentials, whoami confirms identity, and tenant commands handle self-service signup and settings management. These are prerequisites for all other CLI workflows since every command beyond config requires valid authentication.

acceptance_criteria.description: 'loopctl auth login --server URL --key KEY' validates the credentials by calling GET /api/v1/tenants/me and stores them in the config file on success
'loopctl auth login' fails with a clear error if the server URL is unreachable or the API key is invalid (401 response)
'loopctl auth whoami' calls GET /api/v1/tenants/me and displays the tenant name, role, and key prefix (first 8 chars of the API key)
'loopctl tenant register --name NAME --email EMAIL' calls POST /api/v1/tenants/register and displays the returned tenant info and raw API key
'loopctl tenant register' prompts a warning that the raw API key is only shown once and should be saved immediately
'loopctl tenant info' calls GET /api/v1/tenants/me and displays the full tenant profile including settings
'loopctl tenant update --setting key=value' calls PATCH /api/v1/tenants/me with the settings update payload
All auth and tenant commands respect the --format flag (json, human, csv)
The tenant register command does not require prior authentication (it is the bootstrap flow for new tenants)
After successful tenant register, the CLI offers to automatically configure the returned API key and server URL in the config file

test_cases.name: Auth login stores credentials on successful validation
preconditions: Req.Test mock configured to return 200 for GET /api/v1/tenants/me with tenant data
expected_results: Config file at tmp_path contains server='https://loopctl.example.com' and api_key='lc_testkey123'
Output confirms successful login with tenant name from the API response

test_cases.name: Auth login fails with invalid API key
preconditions: Req.Test mock configured to return 401 for GET /api/v1/tenants/me
expected_results: Returns {:error, message} indicating invalid API key

test_cases.name: Auth whoami displays tenant identity
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/tenants/me with {name: 'My Tenant', slug: 'my-tenant'}
expected_results: Output includes key prefix (first 8 chars of configured api_key)
Output includes the role associated with the API key

test_cases.name: Tenant register creates tenant and returns API key
preconditions: Req.Test mock returns 201 for POST /api/v1/tenants/register with tenant data and raw_api_key
expected_results: Output includes the returned tenant name and slug
Output includes the raw API key
Output includes a warning that the API key is only shown once

test_cases.name: Tenant info displays full tenant profile
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/tenants/me with full tenant profile including settings
expected_results: Output includes tenant name, slug, email, status
Output includes tenant settings (max_concurrent_agents, max_projects, etc.)

test_cases.name: Tenant update sends settings patch
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects PATCH /api/v1/tenants/me with settings payload and returns 200
expected_results: PATCH request is sent with body containing {settings: {max_concurrent_agents: 20}}
Output confirms the setting was updated

test_cases.name: Tenant register does not require prior authentication
preconditions: No config file exists (no stored credentials)
expected_results: Command succeeds without requiring existing authentication
The --server flag is used for the registration request
Output includes the returned API key and tenant info

technical_notes: Auth login should validate credentials by making a test API call before storing them — never store untested credentials
The whoami command should extract the key prefix from the locally stored API key (first 8 chars), not from the server response
Tenant register is unique: it needs a --server flag since no config exists yet. Accept server from --server flag or LOOPCTL_SERVER env var
Use Req.Test for mocking HTTP responses in tests — configure the test adapter via Req.Test.expect/3
Settings parsing for 'key=value' format should handle integer values (auto-parse numeric strings) and boolean values ('true'/'false')
All commands should delegate to a shared HTTP module for consistent error handling and response parsing
